builtin_framework: Framewerx (rmi crate) — the built-in agentic-first AI framework

bench_backends.name: file-oracle
bench_backends.purpose: Echo the corpus reference solution. Upper bound on parse rate.

bench_backends.name: perturbed
bench_backends.purpose: Reference + 1 of 8 mutations (drop-`;`, drop-`}`, drop-`)`, swap let/mut, stray `,`, truncate-75%, dup-`;`, swap-words). Simulates near-correct LLM output.

bench_backends.name: subprocess:<cmd>
bench_backends.purpose: Spawn external process per task. stdin=task description, stdout=MechGen source. Plug in a real LLM here.

bench_backends.name: perturbed+refine:<cmd>
bench_backends.purpose: perturbed for propose, subprocess for refine. Measures Stage-3 contribution layered on the perturbed baseline.

ci_floors.name: MIN_PARSE
ci_floors.protects: File-oracle parse rate (100-task corpus) must not regress

ci_floors.name: MIN_HEAL
ci_floors.protects: Perturbed-8 pattern-heal recovery count must not regress

ci_floors.name: refine smoke
ci_floors.protects: Stage-3 refine wrapper protocol must fire end-to-end

ci_floors.name: subprocess echo smoke
ci_floors.protects: Subprocess agent backend must be invocable

ci_floors.name: native-lexer ratio
ci_floors.protects: MechGen text size must stay within 10% of equivalent Rust

cli_flags.purpose: Start the RAP JSON-RPC server on the given addr (default 127.0.0.1:9876)
Dump the complete ontology to disk as static JSON
Reformat source in agent-canonical sigil mode
Reformat source in human-readable keyword mode
Encode RMIL-routed items to a binary RMIB container
Decode a RMIB container back to MechGen view
Decode RMIB and dispatch each item on CpuBackend
Autoregressive generation from a trained checkpoint
Inference over a `train` block's saved weights
Train every `train` block in the module
Forward pass without training over RMIL items
Select hardware accelerator for dispatch (default: cpu). See ontology.hardware_accelerators for the catalog.

Register additional backend descriptors at runtime from a JSON file. Stacks with RDX_BACKENDS_PATH env var and ~/.mechgen/backends.json. Schema: [{ name, family, vendor, requires, summary, available_at_runtime, tags }].

docs.purpose: Per-phase architecture history and final-state grid
This ontology as static JSON (agent bootstrap)

External reference: production HW-agnostic driver substrate + per-model accelerator ontology (NVIDIA / AMD / Apple / Qualcomm / Intel / Google / AWS / open APIs). Drill in for model-specific guidance; MechGen's hardware_accelerators section stays at the backend-family level.

effects.name: @fx
effects.purpose: Effect list: enumerate side effects the body may produce. `@fx()` = pure.

effects.name: @req
effects.purpose: Precondition: predicate over parameters; checked at entry

effects.name: @ens
effects.purpose: Postcondition: predicate over result; checked at exit

effects.name: @inv
effects.purpose: Invariant: predicate that must hold across all methods

examples.description: Minimal public function returning a string literal.
examples.name: hello-world
let-bindings
examples.source: +f compute() -> i32 { v x = 1; m y = 2; y = x + y; y }

examples.description: Match on an Option<T> using ?T sugar.
examples.name: match-option
examples.source: +f opt_or_zero(o: ?i32) -> i32 { ?= o { Some(n) => n, None => 0 } }

examples.description: Struct definition with an impl block. Uses @T sigil for struct literal.
examples.name: struct-impl
examples.source: S Point { x: i32, y: i32 } I Point { +f origin() -> _T { @Point { x: 0, y: 0 } } }

examples.description: Iterate a range using `for` keyword (Phase 56: range in iter position).
examples.name: for-loop
examples.source: +f sum_to(n: i32) -> i32 { m s = 0; for i in 0..n { s = s + i; } s }

examples.description: Minimal neural net: one Linear layer (lowers to RMIL).
examples.name: net-linear
examples.source: net tiny { layer fc: Linear(8, 4); forward { fc } }

examples.description: Linear + ReLU chain inside a forward pass.
examples.name: net-activation-chain
examples.source: net mlp { layer fc1: Linear(16, 8); layer act: ReLU; forward { act(fc1) } }

examples.description: Symbolic knowledge base with facts and a rule (lowers to RMIL).
examples.name: kb-rule
examples.source: kb FamilyKb { fact parent(a, b); fact parent(b, c); rule grandparent(x: i32, y: i32) { x } }

examples.description: Agent role declaration with required capabilities.
examples.name: agent-role

framewerx_modules.name: Sequential
framewerx_modules.summary: Apply a list of modules left-to-right

framewerx_modules.name: Residual
framewerx_modules.summary: y = x + f(x); standard transformer block pattern

framewerx_modules.name: Branch
framewerx_modules.summary: Parallel: apply N modules to same input, return tuple

framewerx_modules.name: Linear
framewerx_modules.summary: Affine transform y = xW^T + b; lowers to opcode 0x0002

framewerx_modules.name: GlobalAvgPool
framewerx_modules.summary: Global average pooling over spatial dims

framewerx_modules.name: Dropout2D
framewerx_modules.summary: Spatial dropout: drops entire feature maps

framewerx_modules.name: Softmax
framewerx_modules.summary: Softmax over a chosen axis; opcode 0x0007

framewerx_modules.name: SwiGLU
framewerx_modules.summary: Gated linear unit with Swish; used in modern LLMs

framewerx_modules.name: RNN
framewerx_modules.summary: Multi-layer RNN with optional bidirectional / dropout

framewerx_modules.name: LSTM
framewerx_modules.summary: Multi-layer LSTM with optional bidirectional / dropout

framewerx_modules.name: GRU
framewerx_modules.summary: Multi-layer GRU with optional bidirectional / dropout

framewerx_modules.name: CNN
framewerx_modules.summary: Plain CNN: Nx (Conv -> ReLU -> Pool) + head

framewerx_modules.name: LatentDiffusion
framewerx_modules.summary: Diffusion in VAE latent space (Stable Diffusion family)

framewerx_modules.name: SGD
framewerx_modules.summary: Stochastic gradient descent with optional momentum

framewerx_modules.name: TrainState
framewerx_modules.summary: Bundles params + optimizer state + step counter

framewerx_modules.name: FlashAttention
framewerx_modules.summary: Tiled exact attention with O(N) memory

framewerx_modules.name: SlidingWindowAttention
framewerx_modules.summary: Each token sees only window_size tokens behind

framewerx_modules.name: LinearAttention
framewerx_modules.summary: O(N) attention via kernel feature maps

framewerx_modules.name: MultiQueryAttention
framewerx_modules.summary: One KV head shared across all queries

framewerx_modules.name: CrossAttention
framewerx_modules.summary: Query from one sequence, KV from another

framewerx_modules.name: KVCache
framewerx_modules.summary: Stateful per-layer KV buffer for decoding

framewerx_modules.name: Expert
framewerx_modules.summary: Single FF sub-network the router can dispatch to

framewerx_modules.name: TopKRouter
framewerx_modules.summary: Picks top-K highest-scoring experts per token

framewerx_modules.name: IA3
framewerx_modules.summary: Scale activations rather than add weights

framewerx_modules.name: PrefixTuning
framewerx_modules.summary: Learnable KV prefix per attention layer

framewerx_modules.name: PromptTuning
framewerx_modules.summary: Learnable virtual tokens prepended to input

framewerx_modules.name: Adapter
framewerx_modules.summary: Houlsby bottleneck FF block after each sublayer

framewerx_modules.name: NeuralTuringMachine
framewerx_modules.summary: Controller + R/W heads over external memory matrix

framewerx_modules.name: MemoryNetwork
framewerx_modules.summary: Multi-hop attention over a memory bank

framewerx_modules.name: PointerNetwork
framewerx_modules.summary: Attention as a pointer to input positions

framewerx_modules.name: LLaVAProjector
framewerx_modules.summary: Linear projection from vision to LLM tokens

framewerx_modules.name: PerceiverIO
framewerx_modules.summary: Cross-attention bottleneck on fixed latent array

framewerx_modules.name: WaveNet
framewerx_modules.summary: Dilated causal conv stack for audio

framewerx_modules.name: NeuralAlgorithmicReasoner
framewerx_modules.summary: GNN that mimics a classical algorithm

framewerx_modules.name: DifferentiableIndex
framewerx_modules.summary: Soft attention into a key-value store

framewerx_modules.name: ToolUsingAgent
framewerx_modules.summary: LLM that dispatches to external tools

framewerx_modules.name: WorldModelAgent
framewerx_modules.summary: Plans via rollouts in learned world model

framewerx_modules.name: ModuleForward
framewerx_modules.summary: Pure forward pass; output shape must be non-empty

framewerx_modules.name: OptimStep
framewerx_modules.summary: Optimizer update; step counter strictly increases

framewerx_modules.name: LossEvaluation
framewerx_modules.summary: Loss function: pure, shape-matched, non-negative output

framewerx_modules.name: HybridVerification
framewerx_modules.summary: Hybrid output: verified flag or non-empty rationale

framewerx_modules.name: TrainStep
framewerx_modules.summary: Training step: io-effect-only, increments step by 1

framewerx_modules.name: Hybrid
framewerx_modules.summary: Compose net (neural) with kb (symbolic) for verified output

framewerx_modules.name: HybridOutput
framewerx_modules.summary: Output of a Hybrid: { value, verified, rationale }

framewerx_modules.name: neurosymbolic_qa
framewerx_modules.summary: Neural Retriever validated against symbolic FactBase

framewerx_modules.name: resnet_classifier
framewerx_modules.summary: ResNet-style image classifier (conv stem + residual blocks)

framewerx_modules.name: vae_mnist
framewerx_modules.summary: Variational Autoencoder for MNIST (encoder + decoder pair)

framewerx_modules.name: gan_simple
framewerx_modules.summary: Generator + Discriminator pair for image GAN training

framewerx_modules.name: flash_attention_block
framewerx_modules.summary: Transformer block with FlashAttention variant (exercises P78 mapping)

framewerx_modules.name: lora_finetune
framewerx_modules.summary: LoRA fine-tuning template over a frozen Linear base

framewerx_modules.name: mixture_of_experts
framewerx_modules.summary: Sparse MoE block with TopKRouter and N experts

hardware_accelerators.name: cpu
hardware_accelerators.summary: Pure-Rust CPU dispatch. Always available. Default backend.

hardware_accelerators.name: cuda
hardware_accelerators.requires: build --features cuda. Uses IronAccelerator's CUDA 13.2 libloading path - the build does NOT require CUDA_PATH set, so cargo build --features cuda succeeds on dev boxes without the SDK. At dispatch time `--backend=cuda` calls into the NVIDIA driver via dlopen; install the driver before runtime use.
hardware_accelerators.summary: NVIDIA GPU via IronAccelerator's cudarc-compat layer (CUDA 13.2 ABI). Device acquisition + cuBLASLt + NVRTC. Production-quality wrapper; faster than cudarc 0.19 on host-side hot paths per IA bench.

hardware_accelerators.name: metal
hardware_accelerators.summary: Apple Metal GPU (M-series and Intel Macs with discrete GPU).

hardware_accelerators.name: apple_ane
hardware_accelerators.summary: Apple Neural Engine. Low-power inference on M1/M2/M3 SoC.

hardware_accelerators.name: vulkan
hardware_accelerators.summary: Cross-vendor GPU via Vulkan. Linux + Windows support.

hardware_accelerators.name: webgpu
hardware_accelerators.summary: wgpu-based cross-platform GPU. Same backend works in browser.

hardware_accelerators.name: qualcomm
hardware_accelerators.summary: Qualcomm Hexagon DSP/NPU. Mobile / edge inference.

hardware_accelerators.name: blas
hardware_accelerators.summary: CPU accelerated via vendor BLAS. Faster matmul than pure Rust.

ir_ops.name: cond
ir_ops.summary: if p then a else b

keywords.summary: neural network definition; lowers to RMIL
symbolic knowledge base; lowers to RMIL
agent role; lowers to RMIL agent ops
agent swarm topology; lowers to RMIL
training pipeline; lowers to RMIL compute
evolutionary search; lowers to RMIL meta
neural-net layer inside a net block
forward pass inside a net block

op_families.name: Control
op_families.summary: Control flow: seq, par, cond, loop

op_families.name: Agent
op_families.summary: Agent communication: send, recv, spawn, delegate

op_families.name: Meta
op_families.summary: Meta / introspection: hash, typeof, mutate, decompose

op_families.name: Math
op_families.summary: Elementwise math: add, mul, exp, log, sin, ...

project_layout.purpose: Lexer / parser / heal / recover / rmil_bridge / rap / ontology
Inline `.mg` examples used by parser tests
RMI Rust crate: binary IR, opcodes, codec, CpuBackend
Module / layers / optim / loss / train / specs / neurosymbolic
Modern neural: attention variants, MoE, adapters, quantization, dynamical, energy, memory, multimodal, world models, advanced diffusion
Classical symbolic: logic, solvers, planning, probabilistic, knowledge
Subprocess wrappers for the agent protocol
One-command end-to-end demo of the agent flow
Static dump of this ontology (refresh via --emit-ontology)
CI with floors on parse / heal / refine / token-ratio

rap_methods.summary: Run the 5-stage recovery pipeline; return final source.
Recover then encode RMIB in one call.
Lookup spec block for a symbol.
Format source in agent-canonical sigil mode.
Format source in human-readable keyword mode.
Per-construct token cost report for source.
Apply elision rules to compact source.
Generate capability manifest for a module.
Generate MechGen from a natural-language prompt.
General NL query against the SKB.
Return one named section of the ontology.

recovery_stages.summary: Source parsed on first try; no recovery applied.
Append matching closers at EOF for unbalanced (/[/{.
Splice ()/0/_ placeholder after trailing operator, then re-balance.
Delete the bad token at the parse error position, or its neighbor.
Stage 3: re-prompt the agent backend with broken source + parse error.

format: per item : { name_len:u32, name:utf8, expr_len:u32, expr:bytes }

sigils.summary: if expression, or Option type prefix, or try operator
for loop, attribute, struct literal, or Arc type
break, or logical not, or assert

types.summary: unit type; sole value `()`; implicit fn return

types.name: [T; N]
types.summary: fixed-size array (length N at compile time)

types.name: [T]~
types.summary: Vec<T>; owned dynamic array; sigil shorthand

wrapper_protocol.name: RDX_TASK_ID
wrapper_protocol.purpose: Stable task identifier; set on every call

wrapper_protocol.name: RDX_TASK_DESCRIPTION
wrapper_protocol.purpose: Original task description; set on refine only (stdin carries broken source)

wrapper_protocol.name: RDX_PARSE_ERROR
wrapper_protocol.purpose: Parse error that triggered refine; set on refine only

wrapper_protocol.name: stdin (refine)
wrapper_protocol.purpose: Broken MechGen source the mechanical pipeline could not save

wrapper_protocol.name: stdout
wrapper_protocol.purpose: MechGen source to evaluate. Empty / unchanged input = no contribution.

wrapper_protocol.name: stderr
wrapper_protocol.purpose: Human-readable diagnostics; surfaces as `agent refused: <text>` on non-zero exit

wrapper_protocol.name: exit code
wrapper_protocol.purpose: 0 = success; non-zero = agent refused / no candidate
